objectives: Collect and wear all required Personal Protective Equipment (PPE)
Understand the function of the shroud in preventing steel oxidation during casting
Learn the importance of timely shroud changes to maintain steel quality and safety
Perform pre-change safety checks by monitoring ladle and tundish status
Remove worn shroud using the shroud manipulator arm
Observe and support lancing operation to clear solidified steel or debris
Install new shroud at the tundish inlet and secure it properly
Coordinate with control room to resume casting operations safely
procedures: Collect Personal Protective Equipment: helmet, gloves, boots, jacket, aluminized suit, gold plated face shield, leg guard, skull cap
Observe shroud position and steel flow path from ladle to tundish
Review consequences of worn shroud: reoxidation, flow disruption, breakouts, inclusions
Check digital display board for tonnage remaining in ladle and tundish
Wait for slag detector to detect slag and slide gate to close automatically
Hold the shroud manipulator arm from the platform
Press the down button on the manipulator arm to lower it
Press the clockwise button to rotate the shroud
Wait for new ladle to arrive at the station
Move the shroud to the right using the manipulator arm before lancing starts
Hold shroud in place during lancing operation performed by NPC
Wait for lancing operation completion confirmation
Hold the shroud manipulator arm to begin shroud installation
Press the anti-clockwise button to rotate the shroud to upright position
Press the up button on the manipulator arm to move shroud close to ladle inlet
Press the lock button to secure the shroud in place
Use walkie-talkie to coordinate with control room for casting resumption
sopContext.constraints: All PPE items must be collected and worn before proceeding with any work. The slide gate must close automatically after the slag detector detects slag before shroud change can be performed. The shroud manipulator arm must be held continuously during lancing operation. In case of damaged or worn out shroud, the shroud must be changed for a new one and will not be lanced. The shroud is changed after every four to five heat cycles. All movements and operations must be performed in the specified sequence using the manipulator arm controls (down, clockwise, anti-clockwise, up, lock). Coordination with the control room via walkie-talkie is required before resuming casting operations.

pendingPlan.storyName: Ladle Shroud Inspection and Replacement - CCM Steel Plant Training

moments.name: Collecting Personal Protective Equipment
moments.userAction: Touch and equip helmet, gloves, boots, jacket, aluminized suit, gold plated face shield, leg guard, and skull cap from PPE storage area
moments.successHint: Voice-over confirms each PPE item equipped; ChecklistUI displays all 10 training steps with step 1 marked current
moments.spatialContext: PPE items arranged on storage racks at waist to shoulder height within arm's reach

moments.name: Shroud Function Overview
moments.spatialContext: Player positioned with clear view of Ladle, Shroud_Ladle, and Tundish arrangement showing steel flow path

moments.name: Importance of Shroud Change
moments.userAction: Click 'I Understand' button on UI panel after reviewing shroud change consequences
moments.successHint: Button click registered; voice-over acknowledges understanding; ChecklistUI advances to step 3
moments.spatialContext: UI panel appears 1.5m ahead at eye level displaying consequences text

moments.name: Pre-Change Safety Check
moments.spatialContext: Player near TundishControler with clear view of digital display showing tonnage and slag detector status

moments.name: Shroud Removal - Hold Arm and Lower
moments.userAction: Grab ShroudHandle and press down button on manipulator arm control
moments.successHint: Mechanical sound plays; empty ladle animation begins; voice-over confirms arm lowered; ChecklistUI updates step 5
moments.spatialContext: ShroudHandle at waist height on control platform; down button accessible on control panel 0.5m to right

moments.name: Shroud Rotation and Removal
moments.userAction: Hold ShroudHandle and press clockwise button to rotate shroud
moments.successHint: Rotation animation plays with mechanical sounds; new ladle arrives; voice-over confirms worn shroud successfully removed
moments.spatialContext: Maintain grip on ShroudHandle; clockwise button on same control panel as down button

moments.name: Lancing Operation Support
moments.userAction: Move shroud to right using ShroudArms and hold position during NPC lancing operation
moments.successHint: NPC performs lancing animation; lancing sound effects play; NPC voice-over confirms 'I have completed the lancing work'
moments.spatialContext: Move ShroudArms laterally 1m to right; maintain position while NPC with LancingPipe approaches and performs operation

moments.name: Rotate Shroud to Upright Position
moments.userAction: Hold ShroudHandle and press anti-clockwise button to rotate shroud upright
moments.successHint: Arm and clamp rotation animation plays; mechanical rotation sounds; shroud visibly moves to vertical orientation
moments.spatialContext: ShroudHandle at waist height; anti-clockwise button on control panel adjacent to previous buttons

moments.name: Install and Secure New Shroud
moments.userAction: Press up button to move shroud close to Ladle inlet, then press lock button to secure
moments.successHint: Mechanical positioning sounds; shroud moves upward toward Ladle inlet; pneumatic cylinder sound on lock; voice-over confirms proper installation and mentions 4-5 heat cycle replacement schedule
moments.spatialContext: Up button and lock button on control panel; Ladle inlet 2m above at top of Ladle structure

moments.name: Coordinate with Control Room
moments.userAction: Grab WalkieTalkie and press use button to radio control room
moments.successHint: Walkie-talkie static sound; control room voice responds 'Thank you for letting us know. We will resume operations from the control room now'; final voice-over confirms completion and safe resumption
moments.spatialContext: WalkieTalkie positioned on nearby console at waist height within 1m reach
